nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: Andaman serpent eagle
nodes.summary: Andaman serpent eagle（Andaman serpent eagle，学名 Spilornis elgini），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，主要分布于愛德華·布萊思，主要威胁包括森林砍伐、栖息地破坏、小种群风险。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 愛德華·布萊思
nodes.summary: 愛德華·布萊思是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联33种鸟类，包括白颊山鹧鸪、棕腹鹰鹃、灰林鸽等33种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Andaman serpent eagle (Spilornis elgini), also known as the Andaman dark-serpent eagle or the dark serpent eagle, is a medium-sized bird in the family Accipitridae, the raptor family, that is only found in India on the Andaman Islands. It is currently classified as vulnerable and is experiencing population declines

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: {| class="wikitable" !Threat !Description |- |Agriculture |Increasing human populations on the islands require more food and therefore, the habitat of the Andaman serpent eagle is being converted into agricultural and grazing land for crops and livestock. |- |Logging |Since the Andaman islands are known for being heavily forested, they are also of interest to logging companies. This interest is further amplified by the need for more homes and agricultural fields for the increasing human presence

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Andaman serpent eagle is currently considered vulnerable because it has a relatively small population in a limited habitat. Since the species is battling habitat degradation and encroachment from human movement to the larger islands, and logging, it is expected that their numbers will continue to decline over time

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Andaman serpent eagle is currently considered vulnerable because it has a relatively small population in a limited habitat. Since the species is battling habitat degradation and encroachment from human movement to the larger islands, and logging, it is expected that their numbers will continue to decline over time
